_meta.note: Auto-generated by code-style skill. Commit this file. Covers all packages: filen-mobile, filen-web, filen-desktop, filen-utils.

typescript.notes: Use 'import { type Foo }' syntax for type-only imports, not 'import type { Foo }'

notes: filen-mobile and filen-web: relative imports (./foo) are FORBIDDEN by ESLint — always use @/ aliases
filen-desktop and filen-utils: relative imports are allowed
Type-only imports use inline 'type' keyword: import { type Foo, Bar } from '...'

exports.notes: Most files export named items + a default export at the bottom. Store files export both named store and default.

jsx.notes: When a JSX element has multiple props, each prop goes on its own indented line; closing > is on its own line aligned with the opening tag

ternaries.notes: Simple one-liners stay inline; complex/nested ternaries break to multiple lines with ? and : at start of new lines

filen-mobile.storePattern: useCameraUpload.store.ts — named export + default export, Zustand create()
filen-mobile.queryPattern: useX.query.ts — named fetchData + named useXQuery + named xQueryUpdate + default export

notes: Tabs everywhere for TS/TSX/JS — not spaces
No semicolons, no trailing commas — both confirmed across all packages
Double quotes for all JS/TS string literals and JSX attribute values
Arrow functions omit parens for single params: 'x => x', not '(x) => x'
Classes are common for singletons (Auth, CameraUpload, Transfers) — exported as instance
React components use memo() for all non-trivial components; displayName always set
Zustand stores: functional setState pattern with updater functions (fn | ((prev) => fn(prev)))

Never use non-null assertion '!'. Try to handle null/undefined cases explicitly instead of asserting non-null. If impossible, use explicit 'as Type' cast when the type is known to be non-null, rather than '!'.
Use fastLocaleCompare() from @filen/utils instead of String.prototype.localeCompare() for filen-mobile and filen-web — it has better performance and consistent behavior across platforms
Use exposed run<X>() functions from @filen/utils for rust-style RAII wrappers
